summary:
**Theme: building the substrate for an AI-first operating model, not just using AI**

The week's through-line is infrastructure-before-features: almost every session is about making AI participation in the workflow *cheap, governed, and self-correcting* rather than ad-hoc. The cost-aware pipeline (zero-LLM extraction + optional summarization), the lightweight CLI-triggered pattern over a paid API, and the exe+browser packaging decision all share one instinct — default to the cheapest mechanism that works, reserve the model call for where it actually adds judgment. That's the economic core of an AI-first org: agents and automation get budgeted like any other resource, not bolted on by default.

The second strand is making agentic behavior legible and self-policing: the Agent Fit Framework lessons, the live "try it" panel against real guard logic, the self-healing groomer states (ready-to-groom / groomer-blocked), and the convention-deviation rule codified into memory are all the same move — encode the judgment once, so future sessions (yours or the agent's) don't re-derive it from scratch. This is the "memory and standards as infrastructure" pattern: institutional knowledge moves out of your head and into files/hooks that enforce themselves, which is exactly what scales an individual's practice into something org-shaped.

The third strand is orchestration discipline: Build-Test-Iterate-Done with an independent critic, explicit gates (backend/API/UI/data) instead of a collapsed "build" step, and the late-week retro on cutting rework by sketching the model visually before iterating. Combined with the permission-allowlist tuning (turning your own tool-call history into reduced future friction), the pattern is: every loop — technical, scheduling, or permissions — gets observed once and then formalized so it runs with less supervision next time. That's the throughline tying this week to the future state: less reacting to each session, more compounding the scaffolding (cost discipline, encoded judgment, gated orchestration) so the system gets more autonomous and more reviewable at the same time.
